title: Sakshinatheswarar Temple, Thiruppurambiyam.
tagline: Sakshinatheswarar Temple, Thiruppurambiyam. — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Sakshinatheswarar Temple or Thiruppurambiyam Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the God Shiva located in Thiruppurambiyam, Tamil Nadu, India.
overview.description: Sakshinatheswarar Temple or Thiruppurambiyam Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the God Shiva located in Thiruppurambiyam, Tamil Nadu, India. Shiva is worshipped as Sakshinathar, and is represented here by a Lingam. His consort, Parvati, is depicted as Ikshuvani. The presiding deity is revered in the 7th century Tamil Saiva canonical work, Thevaram, written by Tamil saint poets, known as the Nayanmaars and classified as Paadal Petra Sthalam.

topPlaces.name: Masilamaniswara Temple, Thiruvaduthurai
topPlaces.description: Masilamaniswara Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Hindu god Shiva, located in the village of Thiruvaduthurai, located 22 km from the South Indian town, Kumbakonam and 14 km from Mayiladuthurai, Tamil Nadu. It is one of the shrines of the 275 Paadal Petra Sthalams.

topPlaces.name: Pasupatheeswarar Temple, Aavoor
topPlaces.description: Pasupatheeswarar Temple, Avoor or Avoor Pasupatheeswaram is a Hindu temple dedicated to Hindu god Shiva, located in the village Avoor, located 12 km south of South Indian town, Kumbakonam, Tamil Nadu, India. The temple is one of the 3 madakoil built by 7th century Chola king, Kochengat Chola.

topPlaces.name: Vellanjar
topPlaces.description: Vellanjar is a village in the Annavasal revenue block of Pudukkottai district, Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Annavasal, Tiruvarur
topPlaces.description: Annavasal is a village in the Kudavasal taluk of Tiruvarur district, Tamil Nadu, India. As of 2001 census, Annavasal had a total population of 682 with 342 males and 340 females.

topPlaces.name: Annavasal, Pudukkottai
topPlaces.description: Annavasal is a panchayat town in Pudukkottai district in the state of Tamil Nadu, India. Which is a part of Tiruchirapalli District before 14 January 1974 and merged with Pudukkottai district 14 January 1974.

topPlaces.name: Meivazhi Salai
topPlaces.description: Meivazhi Salai is a village in the Illuppur taluk, Pudukkottai district, Tamil Nadu, India. It is a small spiritual community, where the Meivazhi religion arose.

topPlaces.name: Sittanavasal
topPlaces.description: Sittanavasal is a small hamlet in Pudukkottai district of Tamil Nadu, India. It is known for the Sittanavasal Cave, a 2nd-century Jain cave complex.

topPlaces.name: Iluppur
topPlaces.description: Iluppur is a panchayat town in Pudukkottai district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sakshinatheswarar Temple, Thiruppurambiyam.?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sakshinatheswarar Temple, Thiruppurambiyam. is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sakshinatheswarar Temple, Thiruppurambiyam.?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (31 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (35 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sakshinatheswarar Temple, Thiruppurambiyam.?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sakshinatheswarar Temple, Thiruppurambiyam. at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sakshinatheswarar Temple, Thiruppurambiyam.?
faq.a: Stays in Sakshinatheswarar Temple, Thiruppurambiyam. start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sakshinatheswarar Temple, Thiruppurambiyam.?
faq.a: The top attractions are Masilamaniswara Temple, Thiruvaduthurai, Pasupatheeswarar Temple, Aavoor, Vellanjar, Annavasal, Tiruvarur, Annavasal, Pudukkottai.
